category_title: Students with communication difficulties and reactive behavioral outbursts when challenged
minimal_context: The student is in a one-to-one setting trying to tell the teacher about an incident that happened in the playground.
dialogue:
TUTOR: You said you wanted to tell me about something that happened in the playground.

STUDENT: Yes, we were out there and... and then he came over and... and he just...

TUTOR: What else?

STUDENT: He took it and... wait, no... I mean... (Becomes flustered and loses track of the conversation)

TUTOR: Just tell me what happened.

STUDENT: I don't know! I can't say it! (Gets annoyed, becomes visibly upset, and refuses to continue the conversation)

options.A: Tell the student, 'Don't say you can't, you can,' to encourage him to finish telling the story about the playground.
options.B: Reinforce the desired behavior with a high five or a verbal compliment when he attempts to continue the conversation.
options.C: Allow the student to not be rushed, giving him space to stop, think about what he wanted to say, and start again.
options.D: Offer a frank description of his abilities: 'You do struggle to express yourself, but you are great at other things.'
